qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated resource exhaustion via sanitize_ipynb endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes unauthenticated DoS via the /-/api/sanitize_ipynb endpoint with unbounded body reads. The advisory describes a missing authorization check in DeleteBranchPost allowing protected branch deletion. These are completely different vulnerable code paths, root causes, and vulnerability classes.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated user email enumeration via email2user endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes unauthenticated email enumeration via the /user/email2user endpoint. The advisory describes a missing authorization check in DeleteBranchPost allowing protected branch deletion. Different endpoint, different root cause, different vulnerability class.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes a missing protected branch and default branch check in the DeleteBranchPost function (internal/route/repo/branch.go:110-155) in the Gogs Go web application, which allows repository collaborators with Write permissions to delete protected branches via direct POST requests. The two qualifying findings address entirely different vulnerabilities: (1) unauthenticated DoS via sanitize_ipynb endpoint, and (2) unauthenticated email enumeration via email2user endpoint.
evidence: Advisory root cause: DeleteBranchPost in internal/route/repo/branch.go missing protected branch check and default branch check (CWE-863 Incorrect Authorization).
sanitize-ipynb-unauth-dos: Different endpoint (/-/api/sanitize_ipynb), different root cause (unbounded body read), different vulnerability class (DoS).
user-email-enumeration: Different endpoint (/user/email2user), different root cause (unauthenticated database lookup), different vulnerability class (information disclosure).
